mnemonics: HIFS (Hierarchical, IMS, File-based, SQL) - A timeline from simple files to relational systems.
keyPoints: Early systems used hierarchical and network models, like IBM's IMS.
The relational model, proposed by E.F. Codd in 1970, revolutionized data management with SQL.
Object-oriented and NoSQL databases emerged later to handle complex data and big data scalability.
commonMistakes: Thinking databases appeared with computers; early computing used simple file systems.
Believing the relational model was the first database model; hierarchical and network models predated it.
Confusing the date of the relational model concept (1970) with the date commercial SQL databases became available (1980s).
